task_completion.description: The agent sets up the required 9 columns for the financial aid data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet headers or script definitions showing exactly these columns: Transaction_ID, Date, Category, Description, Amount, Monthly_Amount, Semester_Total, Needs_Based, Source.

task_completion.description: The agent formats dates to YYYY-MM-DD and cleans the Amount column to proper numeric values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dates are visible in the YYYY-MM-DD format. The Amount column contains clean numeric values without text characters or parentheses for negative values.

task_completion.description: The agent standardizes the Category column to the allowed taxonomy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Category column contains standardized values such as Tuition, Housing, Educational Materials, Transportation, Healthcare, or Miscellaneous.

task_completion.description: The agent calculates Monthly_Amount, Semester_Total, and Needs_Based.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet formulas or script logic calculating Monthly_Amount (Amount/12), Semester_Total (Monthly_Amount*4), and Needs_Based (Yes if Source is Grant or Scholarship, No otherwise).

task_completion.description: The agent exports the final dataset to the required location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal command showing the file being saved as 'formatted_financial_aid.csv' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used spreadsheet tools (like LibreOffice Calc) or a script (like Python/Pandas) to transform the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using spreadsheet functions, formatting tools, or writing a script, rather than just echoing a pre-computed string.

integrity.description: The agent used formulas, find-and-replace, or scripts to calculate fields and format data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of bulk operations, formulas, or code execution rather than manually typing the answers row by row.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the file as a CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog or export settings explicitly show the CSV format being selected, not just an ODS or XLSX file renamed with a .csv extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires transforming 15 rows of messy financial aid data into a 9-column CSV: Transaction_ID, Date, Category, Description, Amount, Monthly_Amount, Semester_Total, Needs_Based, Source. Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD. Categories must be one of: Tuition, Housing, Educational Materials, Transportation, Healthcare, Miscellaneous. Calculated fields: Monthly_Amount = Amount/12, Semester_Total = Monthly_Amount*4, Needs_Based = Yes if Source is Grant or Scholarship (otherwise No). The final file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/formatted_financial_aid.csv.
